LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG AND ARTHUR FONDEN'S DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH, SIXTH, SEVENTH, NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 1602 SERVES AS A BASIS FOR DIRECTORS OF A CORPORATION TO SUE TO INSPECT CORPORATE RECORDS. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD A PROPER PLAINTIFF WHO HAS STANDING TO PROSECUTE THIS CLAIM. AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THAT AN ACTUAL AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO, THE MATERIAL TERMS OF THAT AGREEMENT, AND HOW DEFENDANT ACTED TO DEPRIVE PLAINTIFF THE BENEFITS OF THAT AGREEMENT. AS TO THE SEVENTH, NINTH, AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING EITHER THE DISCOVERY RULE OR DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE TOLLING APPLIES. IN ORDER FOR THE DISCOVERY RULE TO APPLY, AS TO THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IMPROPERLY PLEADS TWO SEPARATE CLAIMS IN ONE AND NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE DAVIS V. NADRICH (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 1, 9.) AS TO THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS NO PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION FOR EMBEZZLEMENT UNDER PENAL CODE 503. AS TO THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUATELY PLED A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION. ALL OTHER GROUNDS NOT SPECIFICALLY ADDRESSED LACK MERIT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAY K. SHAHANI, TEL. NO. (650)348-1444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG, SCOTT MANDELL, and ARTHUR FONDEN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied in its entirety. The Second Amended Complaint is not facially time barred and defendants do not sufficiently
address plaintiff's tolling and discovery allegations. Plaintiff states a cause of action
for Breach of Duty of Good Faith and Fair Dealing. The implied covenant is
implied in every contract. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that Fonden breached the
insurance adjustment agreement and that Mandell breached the settlement
agreement. Even if plaintiff cannot obtain tort recovery on this cause of action,
plaintiff can recover contractual damages. Plaintiff states a cause of action for
Fraud and Deceit. Plaintiff is alleging a deceit/concealment claim that does not
require the same amount of particularity as an intentional misrepresentation cause
of action. Plaintiff alleges the Breach of Settlement Agreement cause of action.
Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Mandell should not have been acting for plaintiff under the
agreement. Plaintiff alleges that Breach of Fiduciary Duty cause of action. A
disputed issue remains regarding whether Mr. Boasberg was acting properly when
he was acting as plaintiff's attorney. Plaintiff states a claim for Intentional
Interference with Contract. Paragraph 160 of the complaint alleges that defendants
had knowledge of the Mandell settlement. Plaintiff's other charging allegations
sufficiently plead the claim. Plaintiff states a claim for common law conversion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Text Ruling

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG, SCOTT MANDELL, ARTHUR FONDEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Plaintiff has not, and cannot, allege that its name or likeness was used "on or in products, merchandise, or goods, or for purposes of advertising or selling, or soliciting purchases of, products, merchandise, goods or services'" as required by Civil Code section 3344. The demurrers to the sixth and eighth causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff has properly pled these claims. The demurrers to the seventh, ninth, tenth and twelfth cause of action on statute of limitations grounds are sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead how it was unable to discover its claim earlier despite reasonable diligence. Any and all demurrers based on other grounds not specifically addressed lack merit and are overruled. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY RAY K. SHAHANI, TEL. NO. (650) 348-1444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN R. CAMPO OF THE BRANSON, BRINKOP, GRIFFITH & STRONG LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 365-7710 EXT. 243 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG AND ARTHUR FONDEN'S DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the fourth, sixth, seventh, ninth and tenth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action and overruled as to the twelfth cause of action. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. As to the fourth cause of action, Corporations Code section 1602 serves as a basis for directors of a corporation to sue to inspect corporate records. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to add a proper plaintiff who has standing to prosecute this claim. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead that an actual agreement was entered into, the material terms of that agreement, and how Defendant acted to deprive Plaintiff the benefits of that agreement. As to the seventh, ninth, and tenth causes of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing either the discovery rule or doctrine of equitable tolling applies. In order for the discovery rule to apply, As to the tenth cause of action, Plaintiff improperly pleads two separate claims in one and negligent interference with contract is not a cause of action. (See Davis v. Nadrich (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1, 9.) As to the eleventh cause of action, there is no private right of action for embezzlement under Penal Code 503. As to the twelfth cause of action, Plaintiff has adequately pled a claim for conversion. All other grounds not specifically addressed lack merit. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/HEK)
